rxn_class_summary: Ni-catalyzed three-component reductive 1,2-dicarbofunctionalization of alkenes with aryl iodides and tertiary alkyl iodides using TDAE as organic reductant

key_intermediates.name: ArNi(II)-I (A), from oxidative addition of ArI to Ni(0)
Tertiary alkyl C-centered radical (C) from Ni(I)-mediated activation of alkyl iodide
Alkyl-CH2-CHR• radical adduct (D) after addition of alkyl radical to alkene
Ni(III)(Ar)(alkyl-CH2-CHR) species (B) from radical recombination with A
Ni(I) species regenerating Ni(0)/Ni(II) with TDAE
Secondary alkyl iodide (E) — proposed but ruled out as intermediate

electron_flow: TDAE reduces Ni(II) to Ni(0), which undergoes oxidative addition into ArI to give ArNi(II) (A); in parallel a Ni(I) species reduces the tertiary alkyl iodide via SET to release an alkyl radical (C) that adds to the alkene giving radical D, which recombines with A to form Ni(III) intermediate B that reductively eliminates the dicarbofunctionalized product and Ni(I), the latter being re-reduced by TDAE.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: significantly reduced reaction efficiency, consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Radical clock alkyl iodide 38 (hexenyl-type)
probe_reagents.observation: with unactivated alkene, 5-exo cyclization product 39 favored over intermolecular addition to allyl acetate (17%); with acrylonitrile, intermolecular addition product 40 obtained in 45% without 39 — confirms C-centered radical and kinetic competition

probe_reagents.name: Preformed secondary alkyl iodide 41
probe_reagents.observation: no reactivity under standard conditions, ruling out secondary alkyl iodide as on-cycle intermediate (path II excluded)

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric ArNi(II) complex 43
probe_reagents.observation: with acrylonitrile, tBuI and TDAE gave product 24 in 55% yield, confirming ArNi(II) competence

probe_reagents.name: Ni(dtbbpy)I2 + 2-iodotoluene + TDAE
probe_reagents.observation: formed ArNi(II) complex 42, demonstrating TDAE reduces Ni(II) and enables oxidative addition

probe_reagents.name: Control without ArI / without Ni
probe_reagents.observation: no reaction in absence of Ni or ArI; without alkyl iodide no reaction; demonstrates all three components required

notes: Authors favor Path I (radical addition to alkene then capture by ArNi(II) to give Ni(III), then reductive elimination). Path II (in situ secondary alkyl iodide E undergoing oxidative addition) is explicitly ruled out by NMR/GC-MS monitoring and by unreactivity of preformed secondary iodide 41. Alternative ArAlkNi(III) pathway via direct recombination of tertiary alkyl radical with A or oxidative addition of ArI to AlkNi(I) is disfavored due to absence of Alk-Ar direct coupling products. Unactivated olefins fail — a coordinating/directing group is required.
